economic_notes: Approximately 72% of revenue from Government of Canada. First Nation is party to a Remedial Management Plan agreement with ISC. Investments in Wellington group (convenience store, motel, restaurant, gas bar in Sioux Lookout), Wasaya Airways Limited Partnership (passenger and cargo air services), BMSA Limited Partnership (construction projects), Watay Power project entities. COVID-19 impacted operations with staff working from home for part of fiscal year.
notes: Audit is a Disclaimer of Opinion (more severe than qualified) due to detailed program records not being maintained and supporting data unavailable — flagged as audit_qualified=true. The 2021 comparative was a qualified opinion by a different auditor. Consolidated entity includes Muskrat Dam Power Utility and Propane. Nine additional entities accounted for by modified equity method including Wasaya Airways, Wellington group, BMSA entities, and Watay Power project entities. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Long-term debt includes term loans due on demand ($710,576) and CMHC mortgage ($183,414). Deferred revenue is very large at $13.1M relative to total assets, suggesting significant unspent contributions. First Nation is on a Remedial Management Plan with ISC due to internal control deficiencies. OSR calculated as total revenue $21,899,169 minus total government transfers $21,262,887 = $636,282.
